options.displayedText: Step 1 - Select the AML Alert to investigate below
Request EDD
Hey {{currentUser.firstName}}, welcome to the AML Investigation workspace!

Click on "Start Processing" on the Inbox to get the highest priority Alert assigned to you.

Details about the Alert, the Customer, and their Transactions history will be available further down to help with your process.

Hey {{currentUser.firstName}},
Click on Start Processing below to process the first refund request
Go through the Refund Request workflow to Approve, Decline, or Escalate the request
👋 Hey {{currentUser.firstName}}, welcome to the Customer Onboarding workspace!
Edit Case Notes
👉 Click on Start Processing to start reviewing the highest priority KYC Case.
Onboarding Workflow Demo
Once you've completed your investigation, use the Action relevant to the outcome below 👇
Alternatively, you can search and select a specific case from the list on the right 👉
👇 Details about the Case, the Customer, and their Documents will be available further down to help with your process.
👋 Hey {{currentUser.firstName}}, welcome to the Forest Playground!

👈 On the left, the navigation bar lets you explore Forest features by use case (Onboarding, AML, Refunds), or dive into the data coming straight from your different datasources (your app's DB, a 3rd party like Stripe, or your BaaS' DB).

To get started, go to the Customer Onboarding workspace in the Onboarding folder.

👇 Or you can stay here and scroll down to learn more about the basics of Forest!
🌲 Forest Basics
🧩 This is a Workspace - a no-code interface you can build yourself for your teams, to gather all the data and actions you require for a particular use case.

This is a Collection component 👉

It corresponds to a table from one of your Datasources - here the Customers table.

It acts as a Source for other components - select an item from the list to set the context for your workspace.
These are Fields, corresponding to the Customer you've just selected in the collection above.
Delete customer
These are Actions - some are native (Edit/Create/Delete), and you can build your own, based on your rules & business logic.

👆
Your current role ensures you cannot delete a customer!

This is another Collection component, this time using the Customers table as a source so that you can access some Related Data - here the Customer's KYC Documents.

Select one to keep going 👉
Finally, this is a Workflow - it enables you to go through a step-by-step process to gather data, use 3rd party apps, and trigger different actions for each scenario.

🧩 Workspaces let you build an interface gathering everything your team needs for a given use case

💾 Collections let you access records from a particular list, to set the overall context for your workspace

🔎 Fields let you display specific data linked to a record selected in a collection

⚡️ Actions & Workflows let you execute your own business logic to update your data and interact with 3rd party apps atomically, or as part of a step-by-step process
🎉 You're now familiar with the basic concepts of Forest Admin
👋 Welcome to the Forest Playground, {{currentUser.firstName}}!

👈 On the left, the navigation bar lets you explore Forest features by use case (Onboarding, AML, Refunds), or dive into the data coming straight from your different datasources (your app's DB, a 3rd party like Stripe, or your BaaS' DB).

To get started, you can either open the 🧩 Customer Onboarding workspace from the 📁 Onboarding folder,
or scroll down to get familiar with the Forest Basics 👇
🌲 Forest Basics
🧩 This is a Workspace - a no-code interface you can build yourself for your teams, to gather all the data, actions and workflows you require for a particular use case, all in one place.

This is a Collection component 👉

It corresponds to a table from one of your Datasources - here the Customers table.

It acts as a Source for other components - select an item from the list to set the context for your workspace.
These are Fields, corresponding to the Customer you've just selected in the collection above.
Delete customer
These are Actions - some are native (Edit/Create/Delete), and you can build your own, based on your rules & business logic.

👆
Your current role ensures you cannot delete a customer!

This is another Collection component, this time using the Customers table as a source so that you can access some Related Data - here the Customer's KYC Documents.

Select one to keep going 👉
Finally, this is a Workflow - it will guide you through a process step by step to gather data, use 3rd party apps, and chain actions depending on the scenario.

🧩 Workspaces let you build an interface gathering everything your team needs for a given use case

💾 Collections let you access records from a particular list, to set the overall context for your workspace

🔎 Fields let you display data linked to a record selected in a collection

⚡️ Actions & Workflows let you execute your own business logic to update your data and interact with 3rd party apps atomically, or as part of a step-by-step process
🎉 You're now familiar with the basic concepts of Forest Admin

This is another Field, but this time using the KYC Document collection as a Source.

Fields can be displayed in the way that is most relevant for your use case - for an Onboarding Document, the File Viewer widget lets you display the file directly in your workspace 📑
✏️ If you want to make changes to this workspace or get an idea of how each component is configured, enter the Layout Editor by clicking on Edit Layout in the top right-hand corner of your screen.
🛠️ Happy Building 🛠️
👋 Welcome to the Forest Playground, {{currentUser.firstName}}!

👈 On the left, the navigation bar lets you explore Forest features by use case (Onboarding, AML, Refunds, Chargebacks), or dive into the data coming straight from your different datasources (your app's database, a 3rd party like Stripe, or your BaaS' DB).

👇 Scroll down to start getting familiar with the basic concepts of Forest Admin
🌲 Forest Basics 🌲
🧩 This is a Workspace - a no-code interface you can build for your team, to make all the data, actions and workflows required for a particular use case, available in one place.

This is a Collection component 👉

It corresponds to a table from one of your Datasources - here the Customers table.

A Segment is a saved filtered view of a collection - you can use the dropdown (currently set to "All") to select a different Segment and focus on a subset of records.
These are Fields, corresponding to the Customer you've just selected in the collection above.
Delete customer
These are Actions - some are native (Edit/Create/Delete), and you can build your own, based on your rules & business logic.

👆
Your current role prevents you from deleting a customer!

This is another Collection component, this time using the Customers table as a source so that you can access its Related Data - here the Customer's KYC Documents.

Select one to keep going 👉
Finally, this is a Workflow - it will guide you through a process step by step to gather data, use 3rd party apps, and chain actions depending on the scenario. Click on it to try it out 👉

🧩 Workspaces let you build an interface gathering everything your team needs for a given use case

💾 Collections let you access records from a particular list, to set the overall context for your workspace

🔎 Fields let you display data linked to a record selected in a collection

⚡️ Actions & Workflows let you execute your own business logic to update your data and interact with 3rd party apps atomically, or as part of a step-by-step process
🎉 You're now familiar with the basic concepts of Forest Admin

This is another Field, this time using the KYC Document collection as a Source.

Fields can be displayed in the way that is most relevant for your use case - for an Onboarding Document, the File Viewer widget lets you display the file directly in your workspace 📑

✏️ If you want to make changes to this workspace or see how each component is configured, click on "Edit Layout" in the top right-hand corner of your screen to enter the Layout Editor.
Or you can create a new one fro scratch by clicking on "+ Add New" in the bottom left-hand corner.
🛠️ Happy Building 🛠️
